title: The Legend of the Ice Gem
summary: A family of moles leaves their forest burrow for a grand expedition across the treacherous Plain of Ice. Guided by a map provided by the local cartographer, Pisco, they traverse snowy fields and sliding mountains before discovering a hidden, blue-sparkling ice cave. Within its frozen walls, Baby Mole unearths a magical Ice Gem with the power to freeze objects. Exhausted from their journey, the moles consume a mystical potion and chant a secret rhyme to instantly return to the safety of their warm beds.

highlightQuote.text: Bisco Bada Bada Basco. Time to go home Mada Masco.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Are you sleeping?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: I'm in my cozy nook. I'm very cozy and comfortable. I'm very comfortable. I'm very snug and warm.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Well, you know who else was snug and warm? Papa Mole and Disco and Thistle and Baby Mole, because they were all snuggled up in their burrow on the plain of ice. But they had warm jackets and warm shoes and each other to keep warm too.

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Come inside my cozy nook.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Okay, now we have Alexander, thank you.

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: This is called a cozy nook.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: I love you in your cozy nook. It looks so cozy.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: And they also had a map. Now, what was on that map? Oh, the Hilltop House. And Papa Mole said, 'Disco, do you think we should go there, or to the ice mountain that I saw with the spiral slide, or somewhere else?'

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Hilltop home.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Hilltop home. Now, do we know who lives there, or should we go and find out?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Nobody lives there. One character lives there who makes maps. His name is Pisco.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Pisco. Oh. 'What kind of a creature is Pisco?' said Papa Mole.

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: A mole! He's a mole that works on maps.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Oh, well we should definitely go see him then.

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: So he went 'Map, map, map, map, I made my map' and then he went 'vroom, vroom, vroom, vroom' and then when he got to Mr. Mole's ice potion, he said 'Hi mischief moles, hi mischief moles!'

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: And they said, 'Oh hello, who are you?'

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Hi, my name's Pisco. And I brought you a map.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: You did, thank you, that's what we've been looking for. Can you show it to us? What's on here?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: It's hilltop home.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Oh. Is it far away from here?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: That's my house.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Oh your house, oh. What else is on the map? Are there other landmarks like mountains?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Mountains, yes. Like hilltop stays possession.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Oh. And what about this other mountain over here?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Pilpa pouchin.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Pilpa pouchin, wow. Is that a tall mountain?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: No. It's a short one.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: And Papa Mole says, 'I saw a mountain with a spiral slide of ice going round and round it. Is that on your map?'

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Of course it's on my map!

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Oh, what's the name of that mountain?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: The Snerpatio.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Snerpatio? Oh, Snerpatio. Snerpatio, oh wow. That's an interesting name for a mountain. And the plain of ice is big, it has lots of different things in it I'm sure. Is there a mountain made of ice cubes?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Sure is! That's right there in the middle.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: And a big field of snow?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Sure.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: And caves in the ice?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Yes. And there's so much tunnels.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Oh, tunnels too, wow. And there's a king of the ice.

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: The king of the ice? Does he have an ice castle? He does. What kind of creature is the king of the ice? A mole named Snisko Parry Disco!

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: A mole named Snisko Parry Disco? Parry Disco. Snisko Parry Disco. And what kind of things does that king of the ice like to do?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Just snapping and doing king things like making dessert, take naps, and swimming find interesting creatures, example-ing the scuba diving.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Wow. And maybe we could go and visit the castle of the ice king.

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: There's something else. There's other things on my map and I need to tell you all about them. There's a Peibo pouchin lamp.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: What's that?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: That's a very tall lamp.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Oh a tall lamp. Oh. Does it shine its light all over the ice?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: No. It doesn't have light but you can climb it with a very tall ladder.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: I see. And what would we see if we got all the way up there?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Far away places like the distance.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: The distance is everything that's far away. Hmm. And what else is there on the plain of ice? Is there a very cold frozen ocean?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Yes.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: And are there ice statues?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Ice statues, sure.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: And are there giant woolly creatures that live in the ice and tromp around like big polar bears?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Yes.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: And giant fluffy white rabbits?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Yeah.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Wow, the plain of ice is amazing. And is there a place where the ice is so flat and smooth that it's like a mirror and it's a perfectly circular place and you can slip and slide all around it?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Yeah. That's the skating place.

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Here's your map back, it's everything.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Thank you. This is an amazing map.

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Now you may go.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Okay, we will go. And Papa Mole said to Disco and Thistle, 'We just have to decide where of all these amazing places we should visit before we go back home. Let's pick one to go to first. Where do you want to go?'

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: The cave!

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: The caves. Oh, that sounds very interesting and very moley if you ask me. It's just the kind of thing that we like. Oh, here it is on the map.

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Nobody.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Papa Mole said, 'Hah, maybe I was wrong. I thought there were caves here. Oh, but what's that up ahead?' And he poked his head in and said, 'Oh, it's all dark in here.'

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: That must be it!

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: That must be the cave. I wonder what's down there. Hey, did you kids bring your headlamps?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Did you bring your headlamps?

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: And Disco said, 'Yes, I remembered mine.' And Thistle said, 'Me too.' And so click-click, they turned on their headlamps. Who wants to go first? Disco, do you want to go first or Thistle?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Everybody said that!

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Everybody wants to go. And so they just all three plus Baby Mole pushed their way through into the cave and suddenly they were in a huge sparkling empty room underneath the ice. And above them, the ceiling was made of ice and they could see the light coming down through the ceiling.

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Nobody lives here. It's just an empty cave. But I bet we'll find something interesting.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: And so they sniffed around. Hmm, what could they find in the cave?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Keep telling the story, then we'll find out.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: And Papa Mole put Baby Mole down to play for a minute while he explored. And suddenly Baby Mole squealed with delight.

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: I found it! I got it!

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: But what is it? And Baby Mole lifted it up high proudly and said, 'I found this!'

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: What's that? It's very cold.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: It is very cold but it's not made of ice. It's a special gemstone that's magic. And when you touch it to anything, you can make it freeze. But then you can touch it again and it will unfreeze again.

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: This is the end.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: And so, did they drink the potion to bring them back home?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Yes.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Okay. And so they took out the clear water potion that Seagraid had given them that would bring them back to their home in the forest. Do you want to make up the rhyme?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Actually you did find the rhyme.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Oh, what was it?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: I don't know.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Oh, I'm going to find it.

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Bisco Bada Bada Basco.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Oh, oh that was it. Bisco Bada Bada Basco. Time to go home Mada Masco. And so they said it two more times. Bisco Bada Bada Basco, time to go home Mada Masco. Bisco Bada Bada Basco, time to go home... Mada Masco. And then pop, they were back in the warm cozy forest right outside their very own burrow.

characters.name: Papa Mole
characters.description: The wise leader and industrious architect of the Mischief Moles, this polite elder visionary guides his kin from the attic’s shadowed heights toward the stars and across biting, crystalline ice. A master pathfinder who initiated their voyage upon unearthing an ancient tome, he balances the weight of celestial navigation with the tactile craft of carving a new tunnel home and the thoughtful devotion of planning whimsical days of activities for his children. Whether introducing surprise robots for play, anchoring meditations during a frozen trek, or sleep-sitting in his favorite chair, his tactical brilliance ensures their furthest discoveries remain rooted in the rhythmic, quiet peace of a sanctuary built by his own seasoned hands.

characters.name: Disco
characters.description: An energetic Mischief Mole and superhero-clad detective, Disco is one of two identical twins who embark on grand adventures through the deep. Alongside her sister Thisco, this architect of the subterranean maps hidden realms—from frozen frontiers to the dusty canyons beneath the furniture—by piloting her signature submarine or racing toy cars through the bedroom's wild heights. Guided by "Cornebyborn" dances and Papa Mole’s lore, she finds solace within her glowing basket house, a dream sanctuary and fortress of a hundred locks. Whether navigating grimy domestic landscapes or open waters, Disco balances mechanical ingenuity with a persistent, soft-hearted wonder for the night sky.

characters.name: Thistle
characters.description: A spirited mole child navigating the biting winds and frosted peaks, one of the small, adventurous souls braving the treacherous snowy climb.

characters.name: Baby Mole
characters.description: The youngest mole, once carried by kin through a grueling mountain ascent to unearth a shimmering artifact of ancient magic, survived the world-scorching fires of a volcanic eruption by sheltering deep within the sanctuary of the burrow.

characters.name: Pisco
characters.description: A map-making mole who lives at Hilltop Home.

characters.name: Snisko Parry Disco
characters.description: The King of the Ice who lives in a sparkling ice castle.

characters.name: Seagraid
characters.description: A character who provides the moles with a magical return potion.

places.name: Plain of Ice
places.description: A vast, frozen landscape where the moles live and explore.

places.name: Hilltop House
places.description: A landmark where the map-maker Pisco resides.

places.name: Hilltop Stays Possession
places.description: A mountain featured on the moles' map.

places.name: Pilpa Pouchin
places.description: A short mountain located on the ice plains.

places.name: Snerpatio
places.description: A mountain with a giant spiral slide made of ice.

places.name: The Caves
places.description: A sparkling, blue underground chamber where a magical gem is hidden.

places.name: Peibo Pouchin Lamp
places.description: An incredibly tall lamp that can be climbed with a ladder to see far away.

places.name: The Forest Burrow
places.description: The warm and cozy home where the moles start and end their journey.
